mnemonics: List operations: L - Loop, I - Insert, S - Sort, E - Extend
Tuples: T - Tuple, I - Index, S - Slice, U - Unpack
keyPoints: Lists are ordered collections of items that can be of any data type, including strings, integers, floats, and other lists.
Operations on lists include looping, inserting, sorting, and extending.
Tuples are ordered, immutable collections of items that can be of any data type.
Tuples can be indexed and sliced like lists.
Indexing a tuple returns the value at that index.
Slicing a tuple returns a subset of the tuple.
Unpacking a tuple involves assigning its elements to variables.
Tuples are useful when data should not be changed after creation.
